page_title: Wage overtime and meal break law — Nevada

meta.title: Nevada Wage, Overtime and Meal-Break Law 2026 | Teamed
meta.description: Nevada wage law 2026: $12/hr minimum (CPI-indexed), daily 8-hr OT trigger for workers below 1.5x min wage (NRS 608.018), 30-min meal break and 10-min rest break every 4 hrs.
target_keywords: nevada wage overtime and meal break law 2026

at_a_glance.daily_overtime_trigger: Over 8 hours/day for employees earning below 1.5x min wage (below $18.00/hr at current floor)
at_a_glance.meal_break_rule: 30 min unpaid for shifts ≥8 continuous hours
at_a_glance.rest_break_rule: 10 min paid per 4 hours worked (or major fraction)
at_a_glance.tip_credit_note: No tip credit — full $12.00 minimum in direct cash wages
at_a_glance.final_pay_late_penalty: Up to 30 days continuing wages at daily rate
at_a_glance.verification_note: Provisional. NV Labor Commissioner 2026 CPI bulletin not yet issued. Confirm $12.00 floor remains current for post-July 2026 payroll.

hero.alt: Las Vegas city skyline lit up at night, Nevada.

NRS 608.019 break mandate (30-min meal, 10-min paid rest)
NRS 608.020 immediate final pay + 30-day continuing-wages penalty

flags.note: NV Labor Commissioner 2026 CPI bulletin not yet issued. Confirm $12.00/hr is still the floor after July 2026 adjustment. The 1.5x daily OT threshold ($18.00) updates in parallel.
